tags.name: ratings
tags.description: Appears to be the "rate the app in a store" dialog.

tags.name: analytics/assignments
tags.description: Assignments are used for feature flagging and A/B testing. They can be assigned to users based on their geohash, allowing for location-based feature rollouts and experiments.

tags.name: browse/blocks
tags.description: Blocking a user automatically deletes the conversation for both of you.

tags.name: browse/grid
tags.description: [Cascade](#get-cascade) returns stuff like advertisements, upsells and partial profiles, presumably ranking by algorithms or paid subscriptions. [Search](#search) returns full profiles, seemingly ranked simply by distance.

tags.name: browse/hides
tags.description: Unknown how it's different from [blocks](/grindr-api/browse/blocks), WIP. Blocks API is preferred until this is figured out.

tags.name: interest/taps
tags.description: Cookie taps are essentially bubbles "hi" but your client can choose to render them as 🍪. There is no separate cookie tap type.

tags.name: media/public-cdn-files
tags.description:
CDN files that are public are accessible directly using their hash, e.g. profile images.

Base URL: `https://cdns.grindr.com`

tags.name: media/signed-cdn-files
tags.description:
CDN files accessible via signed URLs signed with `Signature` query argument. New signed URLs are generated and populated in API responses when existing URL expires. `Expires` query argument holds expiration date in unix timestamp in seconds, 15 minutes.

Base URL appears to be: `https://d2wxe7lth7kp8g.cloudfront.net/`

Although it might change in the future, so it's best to pull full URL from `url` property on media object itself.

tags.name: messaging/albums
tags.description: WIP. No idea what SpankBank, pressie albums and paywalled albums are.

post.description: Make sure you're passing all [Security headers](/grindr-api/security-headers) or you might stumble upon `{"code":28,"message":"ACCOUNT_BANNED","profileId":null,"type":1,"reason":null,"isBanAutomated":true,"thirdPartyUserIdToShow":null,"banSubReason":null}` — but don't fret — it's a fake error, your account isn't banned and API simply blocked your request, not account. Endpoint selection depends on the `recaptcha_first_party` [assignment](/grindr-api/analytics/assignments): when disabled (default) sign-in uses `/v8/sessions`; when enabled the client uses `POST /v9/sessions` with an extra `captchaToken`, sharing the same body otherwise.

reCAPTCHA variant of `POST /v8/sessions`. The client uses this endpoint instead of `/v8/sessions` only when the `recaptcha_first_party` [assignment](/grindr-api/analytics/assignments) is enabled. That assignment is sent by server and resolved before login. The body is identical to `SessionCreateRequest` plus a `captchaToken` (a reCAPTCHA Enterprise token for the `login` action). The official client always populates `captchaToken` here and aborts locally if it can't obtain one, so a `null`/absent token never reaches this endpoint from the app.

x-errors.INVALID_INPUT_PARAMETERS: incorrect credentials; on this endpoint a rejected `captchaToken` (e.g. expired or low-score) is also a plausible cause — server behavior, not confirmed from the client

get.description: Returns the rewarded ad rewards available to the current user and their state. Watching an ad for an `ELIGIBLE` reward temporarily unlocks the corresponding feature (for example `READ_RECEIPTS`).

get.description:
One side will always be the requested size and another will be less or equal to the requested size.

Available sizes for `{size}` parameter:

- `2048x2048` (might be unavailable)
- `1024x1024`
- `480x480`
- `320x320`

Image will be cropped at center and both sides will be exactly the requested size.

Available sizes for `{size}` parameter:
- `480x480` (might be unavailable)
- `320x320`
- `75x75`

Requests the messages from the message id

Regardless of messageId passed, the whole conversation's [`unreadCount`](/grindr-api/messaging/conversations#conversation) will be reset to 0. messageId is taken into account to present the "Read" label to sender.

If you'd like to mark conversation as read but don't show it to other participant, you could pass a valid but nonexistent messageId, such as `0:00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`.

Invalid messageIds will cause HTTP status 400 Bad Request errors.

post.description:
Single endpoint used for both **initial third-party sign-in** and **third-party session refresh**, distinguished by the request body shape (`oneOf`).

**Google (vendor `2`):** the app obtains a Google *server auth code* on-device (`GoogleSignInOptions.requestServerAuthCode(...)`), exchanges it for a Google `access_token` via [Exchange Google auth code](#exchange-google-auth-code) (`POST /v3/users/thirdparty/exchange`), then sends that token here as `thirdPartyToken` (`ThirdPartyRequest`). **Facebook (vendor `1`)** sends its access token directly.

When `registered` is `true`, `authenticationResponse` carries the session and the client stores `authToken`, `sessionId` (JWT), `profileId`, `thirdPartyUserId` and `thirdPartyUserIdToShow`. When `registered` is `false`, the identity has no account yet and the client continues to [Create third party account](#create-third-party-account).

**Session refresh** reuses this same URL with `ThirdPartySessionRequest` (`{ thirdPartyUserId, authToken, geohash }`): the provider token is **not** re-sent — only the stable `thirdPartyUserId` and the rolling Grindr `authToken`. The response is the same `ThirdPartyAuthResponse`, yielding a fresh `sessionId` (JWT) and a rotated `authToken`.

Creates a Grindr account for a third-party identity, used when [Login via third party](#login-via-third-party) returns `registered: false`. The endpoint version is chosen from the first-party reCAPTCHA [assignment](/grindr-api/analytics/assignments): reCAPTCHA disabled → `POST /v7/users/thirdparty` (this endpoint); reCAPTCHA enabled → `POST /v8/users/thirdparty`, or `POST /v8/dev/users/thirdparty` when a dummy captcha token is used. The body is `CreateThirdPartyAccountRequest` in all variants.

First step of the **Google** sign-in flow. The app sends the Google *server auth code* (`serverAuthCode` from the native Google Sign-In result, requested against server client id `1036042917246-68g7siev1clho9lhqevahao9hlbpvssf.apps.googleusercontent.com`). The backend holds the matching web-client secret, performs the authorization-code → token exchange with Google, and returns the Google OAuth tokens. The client keeps `access_token` and passes it as `thirdPartyToken` to [Login via third party](#login-via-third-party). Session refreshes do not repeat this step.

post.summary: Request SMS code for password change

x-notes: Body is URL-encoded (grant_type=authorization_code&code=...&redirect_uri=... or grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=... or grant_type=client_credentials).

put.description: The `counterpartyId` parameter seems to be ignored, it's unknown what its purpose is.

delete.description: Essentially equivalent to [Add note](/grindr-api/users/favorites#add-note) with `notes` set to `""`.

targetProfileIds.description: array of strings with numeric ids, max profiles number per one request is 150

put.description: [Profile](/grindr-api/users/profiles#profile) object, fully replaces the current version. This is the only endpoint that persists the free-text fields `displayName` and `aboutMe`; the partial [Update own profile (partial)](/grindr-api/users/profiles#update-own-profile-partial) endpoint ignores them.
x-notes: Because the profile is replaced fully, every editable field must be sent on each request, including fields not exposed in the edit UI such as `approximateDistance` and `profileTags`.

`displayName` and `aboutMe` (plus custom gender/pronoun display text) are run through banned-terms moderation. A successful update answers with an empty body; rejected fields come back in a [BannedTermsResponse](/grindr-api/users/profiles#bannedtermsresponse), and the offending field is not changed.

patch.description: [ProfilePatch](/grindr-api/users/profiles#profilepatch) object, only updates the specified keys. Applies structured fields only (e.g. `genders`, `pronouns`, `grindrTribes`, `tribesImInto`, `profileTags`, `sexualPosition`, and the `showAge`/`showDistance`/`showPosition`/`showTribes` flags).
x-notes: The free-text fields `displayName` and `aboutMe` are silently ignored here even though `ProfilePatch` includes them — they are only persisted (and moderated) by [Update own profile (full)](/grindr-api/users/profiles#update-own-profile-full).

post.description: Correct request's `Content-Type` header is required.

You must ensure thumbCoords's width and height dimensions are equal, i.e. y2-y1 must equal to x2-x1. Submitting non-square thumbnail won't trigger any errors and it will be uploaded to CDN, however attempting to use such illegal thumbnail dimensions image in [Edit profile photos](/grindr-api/users/profiles#edit-profile-photos) will result in it being silently dropped/skipped.

Also there is a legacy `POST /v3/me/profile/images`.

put.description:
Setting both `primaryImageHash` and `secondaryImageHashes` to `null` works. But setting `primaryImageHash` to a hash value while setting `secondaryImageHashes` to null causes HTTP status 400 Bad Request error. It's recommended to just use `[]` for `secondaryImageHashes` rather than `null`.

Supplied images must have square thumbnails, otherwise they will be silently skipped. See [Upload media](/grindr-api/users/profiles#upload-media).

Repeating `primaryImageHash` value in `secondaryImageHashes` array will result in secondaryImageHashes's entry being silently dropped from supplied request array. Repeating `secondaryImageHashes` values will result in successfully saving the array as-is to the server, however official mobile client seems to drop repeating media when processing `secondaryImageHashes` response.

delete.description: This endpoint removes photo from your profile as well as deletes the media from CDN.

profileIds.description: array of strings with numeric ids
array of strings with numeric ids

x-notes: Gender and pronouns are **managed fields** — profile fields that aren't hardcoded but pulled dynamically from the server.

get.summary: Get a random "For You" collection image

schema.description: array of integers concatenated with a `,`, see [Sexual position ID](/grindr-api/users/profiles#sexual-position-id)

UndocumentedObject.description: Placeholder for a response or request body whose shape has not yet been documented.

Geohash.description:
Grindr requires geohash to be exactly 12 characters long. <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geohash>

Example: `ezjmgyern222` (Madrid, Spain)

> [!WARNING]
>
> Setting geohash inside of United Kingdom will lead to the account being locked and resetting geohash being impossible until you submit legal documents for age verification.

Geohash explorer: <https://geohash.softeng.co/>

ConversationId.description: String with two long integers separated by `:`, e.g. `"12345678:23456789"`. Long integers are IDs of [Profile](/grindr-api/users/profiles#profile). The order of these IDs is always from smaller ID to higher ID, regardless of who started the chat.

RectFString.description: Array of 4 floats or `nulls`: Bottom edge ("y2") in pixels, Left edge ("x1") in pixels, Right edge ("x2") in pixels, Top edge ("y1") in pixels. When used in query, stringified as follows: `y2,x1,x2,y1`.

x-enum-labels.0: FRIENDLY ("hi" or 🍪 based on client's rendering settings)

password.description: string with password, don't specify if using `authToken`

authToken.description: string obtained from login+password flow or `null`

SessionCreateRequestV9.description: Request body for [Sign in via reCAPTCHA](#sign-in-via-recaptcha) (`POST /v9/sessions`). Identical to `SessionCreateRequest` plus one extra field, `captchaToken`. The client uses this shape only when the `recaptcha_first_party` [assignment](/grindr-api/analytics/assignments) is enabled for the device.

captchaToken.description: reCAPTCHA Enterprise token for the `FirstPartyLogin` action. Non-null for the email + password sign-in; `null` for the `authToken` session-refresh flow. The token is only fetched by device when the `recaptcha_first_party` assignment is enabled.

GoogleAccessTokenRequest.description: Request body for [Exchange Google auth code](#exchange-google-auth-code) (`POST /v3/users/thirdparty/exchange`). Wraps the Google *server auth code* obtained on-device from native Google Sign-In.

code.description: Google OAuth 2.0 server auth code (`serverAuthCode` from the Google Sign-In result).

GoogleAccessTokenResponse.description: Response from [Exchange Google auth code](#exchange-google-auth-code). Standard Google OAuth 2.0 token payload returned after the backend exchanges the server auth code. The client keeps only `access_token`, which it passes as `thirdPartyToken` to [Login via third party](#login-via-third-party).

access_token.description: Google OAuth access token. Used as `thirdPartyToken` in `POST /v8/sessions/thirdparty`.

ThirdPartyRequest.description: Request body for the **initial** [Login via third party](#login-via-third-party) (`POST /v8/sessions/thirdparty`). For Google, `thirdPartyToken` is the `access_token` from [Exchange Google auth code](#exchange-google-auth-code); for Facebook it is the Facebook access token.

ThirdPartySessionRequest.description: Request body for **session refresh** via third party, sent to the same `POST /v8/sessions/thirdparty` endpoint as the initial login. Used once a `thirdPartyUserId` is stored (account created/logged in via Google or Facebook). The provider token is **not** re-sent — only the stable `thirdPartyUserId` plus the stored Grindr `authToken`.

thirdPartyUserId.description: Stable provider user id from the previous login (`authenticationResponse.thirdPartyUserId`).

authToken.description: Grindr `authToken` from the previous session (rotated on every refresh).

ThirdPartyAuthenticationResponse.description: Session payload nested in `ThirdPartyAuthResponse.authenticationResponse`; present when `registered` is `true`. `sessionId` is the same JWT as in `AuthSessionResponse`.

authToken.description: Auth token used for session refresh.

thirdPartyUserId.description: Stable provider user id; persisted and re-sent as `thirdPartyUserId` on refresh.

thirdPartyUserIdToShow.description: Display variant of the provider user id (e.g. email/handle).

ThirdPartyAuthResponse.description: Response from [Login via third party](#login-via-third-party) and from third-party session refresh. When `registered` is `true`, `authenticationResponse` carries the session. When `registered` is `false`, no account is linked yet and the client proceeds to [Create third party account](#create-third-party-account) using `thirdPartyUserInfo`.

registered.description: `true` if a Grindr account is already linked to this provider identity.

thirdPartyUserInfo.description: Provider profile info used to pre-fill account creation when `registered` is `false`.

travelPlanId.description: required for update, ignored for create

lastUpdatedTime.description: unix timestamp in milliseconds, may be `0`

ProfileShort.description: Short profile, combines ProfileMasked and ProfileMin.

ProfileRightNow.description: Right Now status info on a profile.

terms.description: The banned terms that were detected in the submitted text.

BannedTermsResponse.description: Returned by [Update own profile (full)](/grindr-api/users/profiles#update-own-profile-full) when moderated free-text fields contained banned terms. Each present key is a field that was rejected. Empty or absent body means the update was fully accepted.

message.description: e.g. `"Profile is not verification required"` or `"Profile is already verified"`

AlbumExpirationType.description:
Album expiration type.

Previously shared [albums in chat](/grindr-api/messaging/messages#albummessagebody) inherit new `expirationType` settings from newer sharings of the album.

x-enum-labels.ONCE: "View Once", limited by 30 minutes from request

albumNumber.description: integer or `null` if album has expired or was locked

totalAlbumsShared.description: integer or `null` if album has expired or was locked

createdAt.description: string, date formatted as ISO 8601, e.g. `2026-03-27T20:39:00`

updatedAt.description: string, date formatted as ISO 8601, e.g. `2026-03-27T20:39:00`

thumbUrl.description: string, unblurred preview, see [Media -> Signed CDN files](/grindr-api/media/signed-cdn-files)

url.description: string, original file, see [Media -> Signed CDN files](/grindr-api/media/signed-cdn-files), may be `""` if `remainingViews` is 0

remainingViews.description: might be -1; absent if this is your own album

content.description: a single blurred preview content item

AlbumNameRequest.description:
String, may be empty (`""`) or `null`, non-string values are coerced into string.

Maximum length: 255 UTF-8 bytes, which is 255 characters for ASCII strings (1 ASCII character is encoded as 1 byte) but less if you include emojis or non-ascii characters (2+ bytes/one codepoint).

maxContentSizeHumanReadable.description: string, incorrectly uses decimal multiples notation (MB) when in fact calculates binary notation (MiB), so API's `120.00 MB` is actually 120 MiB or 125.8291 MB

maxVideoLength.description: long integer, length in milliseconds (1/1000th of a second)

minVideoLength.description: long integer, length in milliseconds (1/1000th of a second)

expiresat.description: unix timestamp in milliseconds; note: observed key spelling, may also appear as `expiresAt`

chat1MessageId.description: string with UUIDv4, second part of `messageId`

name.description: string, profile name, may be an empty string, e.g. `""`

MessageType.description: Message type discriminator. There also appears to be a related `chat1Type`, could be legacy type.

AlbumMessageBody.description: Body for `"Album"` message type. Includes everything from AlbumPreview and AlbumExpiration.

ownerProfileId.description: number or `null` if album has expired or was locked
integer or `null` if album has expired or was locked

previewUrl.description: string or `null` if album has expired or was locked, see [Signed CDN files -> Chat media](/grindr-api/media/signed-cdn-files#chat-media)

AlbumContentReactionBody.description: Body for `"AlbumContentReaction"` message type. Implies "🔥" reaction as there does not appear to be any choice.

AlbumContentReplyBody.description: Body for `"AlbumContentReply"` message type. Extends AlbumContentReaction.

contentType.description: string or `null` if album has expired or was locked

length.description: number in milliseconds (1/1000th of a second) or `null`

VideoMessageBody.description: Body for `"Video"` message type. Additionally, for expiring videos `viewsRemaining` is set; capped at `2147483647` for "unlimited" views.

stillPath.description: single frame, URL to gif file

GiphyBody.description: Body for `"Giphy"` message type. URLs point at `https://media0.giphy.com`.

ImageMessageBody.description: Body for `"Image"` message type. Additional fields are present only for regular (non-expiring) images.

ExpiringImageBody.description:
Body for `"ExpiringImage"` message type. Extends [Image](#image) but with `url` set to `null` and additional `viewsRemaining`.

To get a single-use expiring image URL, you need to call [Get a single message in a conversation](/grindr-api/messaging/messages#get-a-single-message-in-a-conversation). After one call, `url` in message body will be replaced with `null` and image will no longer be viewable.

ProfilePhotoReplyBody.description: Body for `"ProfilePhotoReply"` message type. Unknown, WIP.

RetractMessageBody.description: Body for `"Retract"` message type. Unknown, WIP.

result.description: string or `null`, appears to have the following values: `SUCCESSFUL`, `Duration:`, `Busy`, `BUSY`, `Cancelled`, `Declined`, `DECLINED`, `Missed`, `AB_Unsupported`, `No_Answer`, `UNANSWERED`, `Lite_Unsupport`

VideoCallMessageBody.description: Body for `"VideoCall"` message type. WIP. Only for "status" messages.

messageId.description: string, appears to be a unix timestamp in milliseconds and UUIDv4 separated by `:`, e.g. `"1774296692000:843daee8-1e93-47d6-bc7f-3d981925a393"`

timestamp.description: unix timestamp in milliseconds, appears to be same as in `messageId`

unsent.description: boolean, if this is true, `body` is set to `null`

lastReadTimestamp.description: unix timestamp of recipient's side in milliseconds or `null`; is not affected by yours calls to [mark messages as read](/grindr-api/messaging/conversations#mark-messages-as-read)

profile.description: object if `profile` query parameter is set to `true` or `null`

x-property-groups.heading: Additional body fields for websocket only:

messageId.description: string, must be sent by you

data.description: object, shape depends on `type` — [full_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#full_profile_v1), [partial_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#partial_profile_v1), [advert_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#advert_v1), [top_picks_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#top_picks_v1), [explore_aggregation_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#explore_aggregation_v1), [boost_upsell_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#boost_upsell_v1), [unlimited_mpu_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#unlimited_mpu_v1), [xtra_mpu_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#xtra_mpu_v1), [fav_header_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#fav_header_v1), [hidden_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#hidden_profile_v1), [smart_boost_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#smart_boost_profile_v1), [sponsored_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#sponsored_profile_v1), [braze_event_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#braze_event_profile_v1), [favs_xtra_upsell_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#favs_xtra_upsell_v1), [favs_unlimited_upsell_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#favs_unlimited_upsell_v1), [favorites_header_no_free_results_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#favorites_header_no_free_results_v1), [favorites_header_no_xtra_results_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#favorites_header_no_xtra_results_v1), [profile_hide_status](/grindr-api/browse/grid#profile_hide_status). For `profile_hide_status`, `count` is on the item itself, not under `data`.

items.description: array of objects. `@type` determines contents: *`"ExploreAggregationItem$Location"`*: `data.onlineCount` (integer), `data.uuid` (UUIDv3 string), `data.location` (`id` integer, `name` string e.g. `"Minneapolis"`, `suffix` string e.g. `"🇺🇸"`, `lat` float, `lon` float), `data.profiles` (array of objects with `profileImageUrl` URL string). *`"ExploreAggregationItem$Cta"`*: empty.

SmartBoostProfileV1.description: Smart boost profile. Same shape as [full_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#full_profile_v1).

alternativeProfile.description: Fallback [full_profile_v1](/grindr-api/browse/grid#full_profile_v1) shape, shown when the sponsored ad is unavailable.

onlineUntil.description: unix timestamp in milliseconds, may be absent

ProfileHideStatus.description: Unlike other cascade items, `count` sits directly on the item alongside `type`, not under `data`.

name.description: unique identifier consisting of alphanumeric characters, hyphens and underscores

expiredTime.description: unix timestamp in milliseconds, may be `0`

HivStatus.description: HIV status. Not to be confused with [Sexual health](/grindr-api/users/profiles#sexual-health).

SexualHealth.description: Sexual health (also "Health Practices"). Not to be confused with [HIV status](/grindr-api/users/profiles#hiv-status).

SessionId.description:
JWT obtained from authentication flow. Decoded JWT content:

Headers claims structure:

- `kid` — key ID
- `alg` — `"RS256"`
- `typ` — `"JWT"`

Payload claims:

- `exp` — number, unix timestamp in seconds defining token expiration date
- `profileId` — string with numbers, account's ID
- `roles` — unknown array, appears to be empty
- `features` — array of strings, e.g. `"HidePremiumStore"`, `"CreateVideoCall"`, `"VideoCallDailyFree"`
- `featureFlags` — array of strings, e.g. `"profile-insights"`, `"online-until-updates"`, `"enable-account-filters-bulk-exposure"`, `"a-list-v3"`, `"discover-v2"`, `"boost_purchase_fixes"`, `"discover-studfinder-upsell"`
- `experiments` — object with experiment name → variant string, known keys:
  - `explore-paywall-profiles` — string, e.g. `"test"`
  - `limiting_chat_credits_from_unlimited_to_5_for_xtra_users_in_explore` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `validation-aa-backend-profileid` — string, e.g. `"test"`
  - `grindr-core-day-pass` — string, e.g. `"control"`
  - `llm-age-verification-methods` — string, e.g. `"variant_b"`
  - `trans_tribe_filtering_changes` — string, e.g. `"Test"`
  - `green_dot_v2` — string, e.g. `"treatment-1"`
  - `sponsored-profiles-cascade-selection-mode` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `read-receipt-ad-reward` — string, e.g. `"variant_2"`
  - `taps-paywall` — string, e.g. `"treatment-1"`
  - `explore-insertables-v1` — string, e.g. `"treatment-insertables-below-mpu"`
  - `reduce_number_of_free_profiles_from_99_to_90` — string, e.g. `"test"`
  - `cascade-mpu-explore-studfinder-2026-03-12` — string, e.g. `"control"`
  - `reduce_number_of_results_with_age_filter_for_free_users` — string, e.g. `"test"`
  - `mpu-heuristic-algorithm-optimizations-q125` — string, e.g. `"control"`
  - `ships_in_the_night_v3` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `cascade-mpu-disable-poc-heuristic-2026-03-02` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `for-you-recsys-v1_1` — string, e.g. `"control"`
  - `cascade-mpu-studfinder-profile-limit-2026-03-12` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `reduce_free_user_results_with_any_filter` — string, e.g. `"control"`
  - `cascade-mpu-studfinder-unlimited-2026-02-02` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `mpu-rest-of-world` — string, e.g. `"treatment"`
  - `top_18_mpu_profiles_are_online_in_extended_cascade_and_explore` — string, e.g. `"control"`
  - `for-you-v2` — string, e.g. `"Test"`
  - `rewarded-ads-viewed-me-v2` — string, e.g. `"variant_1"`
  - `mega-boost-v1` — string, e.g. `"mega-boost-low"`
- `systemTime` — unix timestamp in milliseconds
- `upsells` — unknown object, appears to be empty
- `restrictionReason` — unknown value, appears to be `null`
- `grit` — unknown UUIDv4 string

MediaState.description: Public media in Grindr undergo through an automated moderation check before they appear in profile. State can be either `null` for medias uploaded privately ([chats](/grindr-api/messaging/albums#upload-media-to-an-album)) or one of the below for public-facing media.

AlbumCoverUrl.description:
String with URL or `null`, blurred downscaled preview.

JPEG photo with the first frame of video in case of video files.

Becomes unavailable (`AccessDenied`) after album has expired.

See [Media -> Signed CDN files](/grindr-api/media/signed-cdn-files).

CascadeResponseProfile.description: Profile data returned in cascade items.

sexualPosition.description: integer, see [Sexual position ID](/grindr-api/users/profiles#sexual-position-id), may be absent

ExpiringAlbumV2Body.description: Body for `"ExpiringAlbumV2"` message type. For [AlbumExpirationType](/grindr-api/messaging/albums#albumexpirationtype) = `ONCE` but might have other values if expiration settings were changed later. Extends [ExpiringAlbum](#expiring-album-body).

NonExpiringVideoBody.description: Body for `"NonExpiringVideo"` message type. Unknown, WIP.

GenerativeBody.description: Body for `"Generative"` message type. Unknown, WIP.

ProfileLinkBody.description: Body for `"ProfileLink"` message type. Unknown, WIP.

RewardedAdType.description: Feature that can be temporarily unlocked by watching a rewarded ad.

x-enum-labels.LAST_VIEWED_ME_PROFILE: Reveal the most recent profile that viewed you

RewardedAdRewardStatus.description: Current state of a rewarded ad reward for the requesting user.

x-enum-labels.ELIGIBLE: The user may watch an ad to activate this reward
x-enum-labels.ACTIVE: The reward is currently unlocked; see `remainingTime`
x-enum-labels.EXHAUSTED: The reward has been used and is not currently available

RewardedAdInterval.description: Unit of time that `duration` is measured in.

RewardedAdConsumptionStatus.description: Lifecycle status reported while consuming a rewarded ad.

x-enum-labels.COMPLETED: The ad finished and the reward should be granted

RewardedAdReward.description: State of a single rewarded ad reward. All fields are nullable.

duration.description: Length of the unlock window, measured in `interval` units (e.g. `1` with interval `HOURS`).

remainingTime.description: Time remaining before an `ACTIVE` reward expires. `null` unless the reward is active.

countdownTime.description: Time remaining before an `EXHAUSTED` reward becomes `ELIGIBLE` again. `null` unless applicable.
